claim_boundary: Local reduced-order control latency evidence; not physical HIL, FPGA, plant CODAC, or actuator hardware timing.

digital_twin_control_latency.claim_boundary: Reduced-order sensor-to-control benchmark with a host-side simulated HIL scaffold. This section does not certify physical HIL, FPGA, plant CODAC, or actuator hardware timing.

measurement_context.claim_boundary: Python CPU and Rust rows are local non-isolated wall-clock measurements. GPU rows are accelerator measurements only when status is measured. HIL rows are simulated host ADC/DAC timing unless hardware_status names a physical rig.
measurement_context.accelerator_isolation_note: CUDA device was operator-reserved for this benchmark run; CPU host process isolation is recorded in host_before/host_after.
measurement_context.warmup_policy: No samples discarded; deterministic synthetic stream is measured from cold local process state.

host_before.claim_boundary: Local wall-clock regression evidence only; not an isolated-core or hardware-in-the-loop latency claim.

host_after.claim_boundary: Local wall-clock regression evidence only; not an isolated-core or hardware-in-the-loop latency claim.

gpu.claim_boundary: Local non-isolated CuPy/CUDA measurement of the same reduced-order sensor-to-control contract; includes host snapshot assembly and output serialisation.

hil.claim_boundary: Measured host-side simulated ADC/DAC sensor-to-actuator scaffold; not a physical HIL rig, FPGA bitstream, plant CODAC, or actuator hardware timing claim.

gpu.claim_boundary: Local non-isolated CuPy/CUDA measurement of the same reduced-order sensor-to-control contract; includes host snapshot assembly and output serialisation.

actuator_count_scaling.claim_boundary: Actuator-count scaling measures command fanout and output serialisation for the reduced-order local benchmark. It is not actuator hardware timing.

predictive_horizon.claim_boundary: Forecast timing is a reduced-order local digital-twin rollout. It is not a validated 50-100 ms plasma-instability prediction horizon.
